reviews.review_text: Spent 5 nights here. 3km walk into town. Very Quiet park. Good base for winery's and day trip to Burra if you are into History. Went for a swim in the Heated pool, just WATCH out, had to scoop out 2 large spiders crawling on the bottom of the pool that were still alive. Kinda crazy I had never seen that before. All in All in way very relaxing and the Staff were Friendly.

We stayed for three nights at the Discovery park Clare.What a hidden Gem ,fantastic .We were on a Auction and thought we would be put in a very basic cabin .What a surprise,our cabin was great ,spotlessly clean ,good position,everything we could possibly of needed.We will be back .Staff were great.Polite and helpful.A well laid out Park
Great for the family. Plenty for the kids to do. Plenty fir the adults to do nearby.
Y’all got to give Mark a lesson in customer service.
Excellent customer service on arrival

Basically no phone reception for cabins mid to rear of park.
Foul odor coming from drains in bathroom, had to keep bathroom door closed with window open and drains covered.
Bathroom sink was almost fully blocked.
Lounge was filthy and AC barely functional.
Asked about phone reception at booking as i required decent connection for work purposes, was lead to believe it would be fine. In hind sight the response was rather evasive/vague and they claimed to have not heard any feedback about reception issues.

We stayed at Clare Discovery Park and really enjoyed our time overall. The room was great—spacious and comfortable—and the environment was beautiful. The kids’ play area was a highlight, and the staff were incredibly accommodating and friendly. They even provided first aid assistance and helped with bike servicing, which was much appreciated.

There are a few areas for improvement. The showers could be cleaner, as there was some pink mould in the corners, and a few kitchen utensils had food marks on them. It would also be great to see an upgrade in kitchen equipment, as some items (like the spatulas) were quite worn.

Overall, we had a lovely stay and would definitely come back.

Such a beautiful Discovery Park in a prime location with everything you need right at your fingertips. We stayed in the newer section and loved every second of it. It was such an enjoyable visit that we are now making it an annual tradition for us. Thank you for an amazing stay!

Unfortunately have to say I’m disappointed. Meant to stay 5 nights here however I’m currently searching for alternative options for the following reasons: wifi drops out and internet is slow (here on business so this matters). The couch was filthy, AC issues and a funky odor. Wouldn’t be a bad place for a few nights camping but overall I wouldn’t stay here again
Great place for the family. Right on the highway for convenience but ca get a bit loud.

Clare - Discovery Park.
We visited in March 2026, 2 nights in a Cabin, a base for our Clare experience.
The (new) Cabin was neat, tidy and comfortable!
The Park is very organised, neat and clean.
(We have stayed an many G'Day / Discovery Parks on our road-trips).
Service was excellent and warm - Thank you.
The Camp Kitchen was especially good!
We enjoyed our two days there.
The Park is very central to lot of attractions and things-to-do in Clare region.
Yes, well recommended.
Thank you.

Fantastic holiday park, plenty for young and old to do, movie nights for kids, live music, plenty of sports equipment and games set around the place, Amazing staff. Great location.  These guys clearly work hard to make your stay enjoyable.

Great location and good facilities onsite, great for families, wasn't great for us with wanting to get to sleep early with a running carnival the next morning, but I would definitely go again at another time. Shout-out to the worker crashing and bashing behind the check in desk when I was trying to give my details to check in with another worker.
Nice park outlook, hot showers. Only fault is main bedroom so small (only few inches around bed.)
We had a Travel Auction stay here it had everything we needed for a pleasant stay & quiet.
This is a family friendly park, safe for kids with good amenities and close to everything.

This caravan park is excellent. So much for kids to do with two pools and a splash Park. an amazing part of the world to explore. Kids bathrooms are a godsend also. Makes camping easy and fun. We cannot wait to come back!!

Clare was the last stop on our two week SA motorhome trip. After camping at a variety of campgrounds along the way,  Clare Valley Discovery Park exceeded all expectations.||We were fortunate to be assigned a site in the newly renovated section of the park with green, grassy sites surrounded by beautiful gum trees. The Kookaburras and birdlife were abundant.||Our site was very close to an exit through the Wine Food & Tourism Centre, which made accessing the Riesling Trail (approx 300m away) quite easy - whether on bike or on foot.||Clare Valley Discovery Park offer numerous camping options from tents to powered caravan/motorhome sites - to new cabins.||The amenities are very modern and clean. ||The laundry facility closest to our site had three washing machines and three dryers. No coins needed, just tap to pay and very straight forward to use.||If you are looking for a great place to spend a few days in Clare, we would not hesitate to recommend you choose Clare Valley Discovery Park.||Great amenities|Close to Riesling Trail|Next to Info. Wine & Tourism Centre|Close to town of Clare|E-bike hire on site or through Riesling Trail Bike Hire|Great for kids - onsite playground
Lots of things for kids to do.
Great caravan Park
Location spot on to explore.  Staff helpful in suggestions to see best of Clare.  Sites awesome. Great clean new amenities.

Unfortunately it poured rain the whole time we were here, resulting in our site flooding. But it was still warm, so we had a good play with the kids in the pools and splash park. Lots of kid friendly activities, great amenities. A fair walk into town but still not too bad

Was supposed to stay here for 4 nights but didnt as the site we were put on had bees swarming everywhere and the site was definitely way to small for our van also one of the pools were out of order at the time of check in, went back to reception for a refund was informed they dont do refunds can only move us to another site, as they obviously didnt have anything left other then drive thru sites (and didnt want to upgrade us) gave us a full refund that took 5 days to come back. Has definitely left a sour taste in our mouth won't be be back dont recommend and probably will avoid all discovery parks in the future.

The cabin room was full of hair and the sheets full of dandruff and hair the pillow Cases were yellow and the shower was full of hair as well as the toilet floor but everything else was ok but cmon guys u can do better

WASPS everywhere. A ridiculous amount of wasps, swarms that bothered us at the BBQ area, on our decks, the pools, the water park. Not just 1 or 2. Whole swarms. There are signs warning of this. However, no traps were set around those areas, which is not good enough. Our 3 year old got stung, anaphylaxis is a huge concern.
If you have anaphylaxis wasp allergies, I would strongly encourage you to look elsewhere for safe accommodation.
This is very dangerous and should be dealt with with adequate pest control.

The amenities and stuff for the kids were great, but definitely ruined by the wasps that should have been controlled appropriately.

The cabin floors were caked in dirt, the ceiling fan was also covered in a thick layer of dust so it was unable to be used. It's a bit ridiculous considering if you leave the place "untidy" you get stung a $500 cleaning fee. Clean your rooms properly it's disgraceful.

Beautiful 😍 park with lots of shady trees. The pool area and out door eating area is huge and spectacular.
The staff were super friendly and so helpful. Lots of nice places to see in Clare too.
Great caravan park,  large sites,   great amenities,   clean and well priced,
Beautiful grassy camping sites, lovely landscaping and immaculate well designed amenities.

Our second time caravanning at Discovery Parks Clare on a drive through powered site. We absolutely love this park. It’s well set out. The facilities are all close by. The showers are the best. The swimming pools, bounce pad, bike track and splash park are brilliant. Huge undercover cooking area with bbqs and pizza ovens. Nice clean well maintained grounds. Wifi as well. Thank you so much.

Had a very relaxing trip. Bec running the craft everyday was a lifesaver and the treasure hunts kept the kids occupied for hours.

The activities and facilities have been great!

Had a great stay at the park the facilities are amazing and very clean , the kids loved the water park and heated pool and Bec the park supervisor was super friendly and bubbly which made our stay a whole lot better giving us all the hot tips from free wine tasting and the free bbq and other things happening in and around the park , 5 stars from us  🤠
A little out from the main part of town. A little traffic noise. Management have developed the park considerably with a new section of flash cabins opening up. Well treed site.
Nice park. Great pool area and separate kids water area with water slides. We stayed in a tent on a powered site. Amenities old but clean. The park is well kept. We enjoyed our weeklong stay.
Nice Cabin and quiet overnight. Traffic on highway thru the day. Cabin is clean and bed comfortable.

No hot water anytime I went to use the showers or for my partner. Rang 10.30am in cubicle and graham said to wait a minute and try again... Nope. Lady in cubicle next to me had just shampooing her hair.

Very nice campsite in a great location. Booked several months ago and was assured our camper trailer would fit on the pitch but it did not. Good job we could get side on.
The showers were more than disappointing. There were 8 of us in our party and the only 2 who had a warm shower (not talking hot just warm ) were the couple in the cabin.
When I mentioned it at reception there was not much response from the reception staff except that it is costing $75.000 to fix and told to have a shower on a night and not in the morning! During this time of very unreliable hot water you should either let gusts know prior or reduce your nightly price.

We recently camped at this beautiful caravan park in Clare and absolutely loved it! The location is stunning, surrounded by nature yet close to everything you need in town. The park has a great family-friendly vibe, and you can tell it’s well cared for.

All the amenities and facilities were spot on clean, accessible, and well-maintained. The only thing we’d love to see in the future would be a few ensuite sites for added convenience, but that’s just a suggestion, not a complaint.

Overall, we had a fantastic stay and would definitely come back again. Highly recommend this park to anyone visiting Clare!

We both had a wonderful stay at Clare that was amazing from the moment we checked in with Rebecca she had such enthusiasm and warmth as soon as I walk in 🥰.
I am one of those guests that when you ask how was your day did you have a good trip I tell you 🤗🫣
After explaining it was absolute wonderful as it was my husband’s birthday yesterday and our wedding anniversary today and the drive was beautiful 🤩 Rebecca presented us with a gift of a bottle of COLD Mr Mick.
As we were travelling for his birthday it was the only other present 🎁 he and I received.
Thank you ❤️
The check in was as wonderful as the corner well maintained grass site for us and our dog.
All the facilities and staff are
5⭐️
Grounds
Pool
Camp kitchen
Fire place
Laundry
Showers & toilets
Would definitely return for a longer stay
Cheers 🥂
Judy
Good layout friendly staff.

This place is incredible. Best long weekend away we’ve ever had. They have a great set up for both kids and adults. Two pools (one warm, one cool, both super clean), pump track, enormous jumping pillow, playground, splash park. While we were there on the long weekend they also had craft (kite making) and a colour run for the kids. I did the ‘Paint and sip’ activity and it was so much fun. The whole place is beautiful to be in, surrounded by soaring gums. We spent lots of time just relaxing in the shade, chatting with our friends and enjoying the place. There are lots wineries, distilleries and cool playgrounds nearby you can walk, ride or drive to. But it was so lovely at the discovery park we only headed out and about a couple of times in the end. We will definitely be back. Thanks for an awesome holiday.

We stayed during the October school holidays.  Park was very busy and very tight spaces for caravans. Great park but severely let itself down with its showers. Absolutely no water pressure and at times no hot water. 3 shower cubicles each in male and female blocks; even with one person in the shower a 2nd person attempts to have a shower and you’re literally standing under a trickle of water. Worse if opposite sex showers are in use.  Not acceptable given rates charged. Nothing worse than BAD showers when you are in a caravan park. It was discussed by many families staying in the park and no attempt made to fix the issue. The park has really let itself down with this issue.

A fantastic caravan park.  Drive through sites are so good - flat and convenient.
Facilities are excellent - toilet blocks clean and purposeful.
Camp kitchen - huge and useful.  2 electric BBQs and lots of tables and chairs.
Pool is heated and very nice.
Such a fabulous park for the kids, awesome facilities except the showers. No water pressure and you can’t even run all 3 showers at once. This was extremely frustrating for us.

Nice clean park with great facilities.
Ok if you are towards the back of the park, but up near the road there is a bit of traffic noise.

Great showers and toilets. Sites a little tight and roads not wide with fairly sharp turns. I know they are expanding but could have done without a 0700 start on the diggers just behind the site each morning (especially as they stopped within a couple of hours).

After having knee replacement requested site close to disabled amenities,could not have been placed any father away from them.hot to cold showers not very helpful in cold conditions.toilet bowls are so low it is a joke,discovery head office listen to myconcerns more than management at park,it was just all to hard for them

Lovely park to stay and good amenities however it was very overpriced for a tiny powered site|Saying that though reception/check in was good|At nearly $60 per night not even Adelaide prices are that high|Also hot water ran out during my shower |Really disappointing as I have stayed here for years |Not sure I will return
Nice park. Showers were hit n miss with hot water. Kids loved the pool.
A lovely clean Park and helpful staff but the water pressure was appalling for a shower, so don't decide to wash your hair!
Heaps of equipment for kids to enjoy. Plus great kitchens and laundry to use.
Stayed here for a weekend whilst I was visiting family, absolutely fantastic cabin, management and staff friendly as, prices also fantastic. Thanks team I know where I'll be staying again!
Always nice here

Nice park have stayed here a couple of times . This stayed unfortunately the cabin we were in was right next to the kids play area. Bouncing basketballs early in the morning and jumping pillow noise. We have adult children now, I need to make sure when I book to put in the special request cabin away from the kids play area. Will stay again.
Great park. Loved everything about it especially not having those stupid boom gates at the entry.

Great spot to stop with the family, pool is heated, the pavilion with pizza ovens is very impressive. 10/10 waterpark in summer 👌🏻
Showers may need some work in the shower block.
Stayed for a couple of nights on the way to the Flinders. Good spot right near town and good value. Good base to explore all the great local wineries.

I did take the cheaper option room so I can not complain to much, you get what you pay for. Next time I know to pay the extra and book a moderm option.
Cabin I had was run down, dirty floors, couldn't move in the bedroom, shower water filled the cabin floor when showering. Heater was very noisy, vibrated the cabin that much that you could not sleep, had to turn it off.
Apart from that staff were lovely, facilities are good and the park grounds are well mantained

Clare Caravan Park were incredibly supportive when our van was unexpectedly written off. Their kindness and understanding made a tough time easier to navigate. The park is clean, peaceful, and well located - but it's the people who make it stand out. Highly recommend.

Another great park with lots of sites. Would suggest booking as it's usually busy. Camp kitchen with pizza ovens, clean amenities, and dump point. A little way out of town and on the main road. Road noise was not a problem. Check in quick and staff friendly.

Fabulous caravan park... Booked for 2 stayed for 3 nights....all powered sites but so much for the kids to do a great base for all the wonderful local wineries and eatieres.... A park that had absolutely everything you could want...and great common areas and cooking amenities....

Was one of the cleanest and best caravan parks we have stayed in. Gravel drive thru site. Easily accessible. Facilities were spotless. Easy check in/out and staff very friendly.  Would love to go back to Clare and explore further and would definitely stay here.  Have onsite e-bikes for hire to get around to town or the Reisling trail etc

The park is well located to Clare and other services. The toilets were not great with a couple overflowing with paper and crap - literally. Showers were nice and hot but dated. Really good camp kitchen, eating area with an open fire. Sites were very tight and I struggled to fit on one with a medium size van. Price was reasonable but I thought a bit more than I wanted to pay in winter.
Great location,  great prices, nice evening fireplace. Excellent facilities for meal preparation 👌

Lovely park just out of town.
Friendly staff and clean amenities.
One small thing that's a bit disappointing is the way you get packed in like sardines.  Apart from that would recommend this lovely park surrounded by beautiful big gum trees and lots of birds!!!

The June long weekend was the wettest, coldest and most miserable, so let's go to Clare and hang out with friends, see the Superjases perform (yes, you read that right, a duo called The Superjases) in the caravan park. 100 people under shelter is like extended family; wood fired pizza, espresso martini, wine tasting and a sausage sizzle put on by the park staff as their contribution to the weekend. When I mentioned needing an extra quilt it was delivered to our cabin in minutes. What a top crew!

Very good caravan park
Amentities are spotless
Friendly staff
Park has  great camp kitchen and pools

Easily the worst Discover Parks place we've stayed at. We had a standard cabin, which was well overdue for an overhaul and not clean - a big change from the lovely standard cabin we had in Mildura. We tried to cook and the frying pans were filthy,  as was the bathroom. The manager moved us to another standard cabin, which had less amenities. We had to take the frying pans with us and clean them ourselves. We ate with the bugs on the window ledges, dust on the skirting boards and mould in the shower. The wifi signal was so weak, we had to use our phones to watch on demand channels on our laptop in bed because the couch looked too dirty to sit comfortably on. It's also on a busy road. Traffic didn't bother us too much, as we were right at the back, but sites close to the road would be noisy.
Claire caravan parks 1 of the best ones so far

Caravan park setting is great for singles, couples or families.

It has a fantastic undercover eating/entertainment area complete with a huge log fire.

Amenities are clean and today but maybe not enough showers when park is full.

Nice swimming pool and heaps of activities for kids.

The room we stayed in wasn’t in the best shape. The fridge door was difficult to open due to the swelling in the floor. I think the price for this tiny old room was far too much, but the place grounds and pools were great. I also didn’t have the warmest interaction with the staff at reception.

Good location and park busy

Sites ok if you get a level site

We selected a budget cabin with a kitchenette and were very pleased with the facilities and the cleanliness.||In addition to the standard microwave and electric kettle, there was a 2-burner hotplate and a banquet electric frying pan.||Easy access (walking distance) to the Tourism and visitor information centre. Thereafter Paulette Wines, Seven Hills and Martindale Hall are less than half an hours drive.||An added bonus it was very peaceful and conducive to relaxation. This was our main goal.
Camp amenities were good but the cabin was basic and average for the cost. $250+ a night to sleep on very uncomfortable beds.
Nice caravan park, friendly staff, a few more amenity blocks would be better as it's a big caravan park.
One of our favourite spots to go as a family! We love the local attractions, the park is well equipped for families and the new splash park area is awesome! Will come back soon
Nice place to spend whole week developing marsian rover. Emu living nearby.

Trying to book a cabin, but no phone contact possible after 5pm. "Go to website to book".
Website booking is terrible. Does not accept Vic as a state. All details entered, pri e is good, but them NOTHING happens when I hit 'pay'.
Get your damn Web system organised, or else staff your phones till at least 8pm if you aren't going to do that.
Deluxe cabin was more standard level, small and couch very uncomfortable. Good deck

Paid $340 per night for a 2 bedroom cabin. Way too much for a caravan park. Tried to pay over phone prior to arrival- no it’s been paid. Tried to pay on arrival - same. |About to leave - your room is overdue.  Think air bnb in Clare would have been better value.
Nice family park with loads of stuff for the kids.

41 degrees,  grumpy bloke on golf cart closed pool at 7 . Roughly 12 people wanting to use pool to keep cool. One family with 3 hot toddlers! Staff told us before we arrived that pool would be operational, Courtesy bus to pub was not available. Cockroaches around table in Rotary undercover area. One crawling on my drink can. Very, very disappointing! We chose Clare over Burra for the pool . 🤨 People expect more from a well known brand Park. Lift your game Discovery Clare!. Ps would have been  nice if Mr Grumpy on Golf cart waved back when we greeted him on way in.
Great park but closing the pool and water park at 7pm when it's still 40 degrees is retarted, doesn't get dark for 2 more hours

Well kept park, lovely staff, stayed in a deluxe cabin which was small, had to walk side ways to get into bed even in the 2nd bedroom, 1 air con in the lounge and that didn't reach the bedroom much as a wall was blocking it to blow down, did have ceiling fan but they were blowing the heat around in bedroom, bed was comfy. Would stay again but I will wait for the new cabins they are getting
Had 5 days there and 5 more in April close to every where.

The cabins are well over priced compared to other parks around South Australia. For the price of their luxury 2 bedroom cabin, it is very small. There is no room to hardly move in the main bedroom with the queen bed. Forget trying to put a large bag or suitcase in there. The second bedroom has sleeps 4x in bunks. Again no room left for any bags. So the living area will be your storage space, which is also small if there are more than 2x people. The photos on the booking site make it look much larger than it is. The outside ground amenities e.g. swimming pool and paly equipment are good.
Only went to the water park and that was pretty cool. Friendly receptionist

Love love love this place. 3rd time here and it’s been just great 😊 yeah next to the highway, but noise isn’t bad. We don’t have any kids with us, but it’s great to see them all outside having fun 🤩
The facilities are fantastic. BBQs, sinks to clean 🧼 your dishes and unless YOU leave it dirty then that becomes someone else’s problem. Not up to staff to clean up after you. Toilets 🚽 are great 😊 we have a van small enough to be on the grasses area and lots of space (where we are). They always accommodate if you ask. Will be a regular for sure!

We've stayed at this park twice now and our kids always have a great time. Staff are super friendly and lots of shade on the campsites from all the big gums around the park.

We feel though that alot of the amenities are getting a bit tired / rundown and could do with some upgrading - the showers are old and mouldy and didn't get cleaned for 2 days straight during our stay. Shower pressure is terrible and we were told that this is due to their sprinklers being used all day on mains water because there was a problem with their bore. There is also no exhaust fan in the bathrooms so it has a permanent stagnant poo stench. Some umbrellas around the pool would be incredible - 2 of the days we were there it was 38 degrees and in the middle of the day there is zero shade to sit.

After a little glitch with our booking, we were offered an upgrade- the staff were very helpful and accommodating. They certainly went above and beyond. Park had fabulous facilities for the kids and probably the best one we have stayed at. Highly recommend.

More of a parking lot than a campground. The space is quite terrible, with sites stacked next to each other, almost no tree or vegetation and toilet blocks and amenities are a rarity, with 2/3rd out of order.
Clearly a park which is no longer maintained, except for the pool maybe.
Great location, next to the highway... but also really next to the highway!

Thank you so very much for an amazing 2 nights stay. Wish we could have stayed longer. Kids had an absolute ball. Staff were amazing, facilities were amazing. Thank you once again. The best Caravan park I've come across so far in Australia.

Kids were all very happy. Ranging from ages 2 to 14.

Can not fault this place.
Well done everyone.

If we can we will be back in the future.
Yes all the way from WA
Lucky enough to win 10 nights through Travel Auctions. Had 2, 5 night stays.clean amenities, pet friendly, playground, jumping bag,2 pools,under covered BBQ area.
one of the best parks we have come across
Fantastic park, gravel and grass sites, heaps of cabins, plenty of trees, a great place if you have kids.
It was nice experience with staff, they have wide range of activities to spend quality time with the family. Room facilities are great and kitchen is fully equipped with the all Amy
Great caravan park, very quiet clean facilities and friendly helpful staff. Close to good wineries
Great park for children
Wonderful facilities with many things to entertain the kids. The Gasmate pizza ovens are great.

Unfortunately I had a terrible experience with the Bike hire here, the staff at the location gave us unsuitable bikes (a small bike for a 6 ft adult with no choice of anything better) and charged us twice what we should be ($70 a day per bike!)

We were forced to make our way to their bike supplier to change bikes, and if we went to them straight away we would have been charged half the rate ($35 a day). We left feeling like we had been ripped off and wasted our saturday morning. I would recommend avoiding bike hire here.
A lovely park. Great for the kids and has two nice pools. One heated one not. A waterpark, mini BMX track, jumping trampoline and playgrounds for the kids .

This was our first trip to Clare and it was perfect! Our cabin was clean and had enough space for our family. It was in an excellent location to watch the children play while we sat on the balcony and we were really happy with the low noise level during our stay.

The park was clean and well maintained. The facilities were great! We used the BBQ area a few times, and it was a great place to sit and have our meal. My children loved the large bouncy pillow, water park, playground, pump track and the pool. We soon discovered the box between the basketball court and pump track that stored balls and cricket sets. This was a wonderful surprise and my children really appreciated them.

On top of all this, the absolute best part of our stay were the interactions with the people who work there. My children frequently approached them and asked all sorts of questions, and they always took the time to answer and everyone was very kind. The reception staff were amazing and very approachable. There was a sign in the laundry about drawing a picture of the park and showing it to reception. My daughter took up this opportunity and the joy and praise the staff share at seeing her picture absolutely made her day. It was a really lovely experience.

We have been to several caravan and cabin parks now, and have loved them all, but Clare really stepped up and we are so impressed. We will definitely be staying there again. Thank you so much!

Overnight stop as we are travelling through, powered site, 2 adults, November, booked by phone as online system said no powered sites available for a single night, some cited 2 night minimum, however there were certainly heaps of vacant sites evident when we got there.No dedicated turning lane when coming from the town side and checking in around 12 saw vans and motorhomes queued.
The park itself is beautifully set in amongst gum trees with plenty of grassed area, sites were gravel and of a reasonable size.  The drive through sites opposite us were all fairly large with good access. Amenities nearest us (site 25) had inexplicably low set toilets leaving me perplexed, no signage to advise, one had a support bar on the wall, the next cubicle had one at some stage. Certainly not a comfortable height. Showers were hot with plenty of pressure.
Great staff in reception and it’s definitely worth staying when visiting in the area.
Great park. Very neat n tidy. Excellent facilities for children. 2 pools, camp kitchen is fantastic. Highly recommend staying here

What a fabulous place to base yourself in at the Clare Valley. Everyone was friendly and helpful. Our site was quiet, and amenities were great and very clean. We stayed for a week and could have stayed longer. Lots of places to explore and some wineries in walking distance from the park 👍
Great caravan park ,  staff are fantastic , great for families so much to do   We loved it and were lucky enough to have the Clare show on while we were there

We haven’t stayed at the park yet, however I call and call and get no answer. 4 days I e been calling and all at various times of the day each day, still no answer! It’s seems they don’t want to take bookings at all! I left voice message as instructed and I get no call back, nothing at all. It’s very disappoint and we haven’t even stayed yet!!!! If our friends who we are intending to go with hadn’t already booked I would not bother at all. Worst experience already!!!

Good luck to others!
So much fun places here there 2 pools and a water park
Great facilities but unpowered area needs a toilet block.
Nice place for kids would go back

We stored our caravan in the park for a few days while we flew home for a few family matters and actually only spent a few days there
The park is very nice with good facilities and is adjacent to the local visitors centre

The dishes had stains in them (like dried food?) and there was something in the bedrooms that gave me sever allergy. The rooms could use a good dusting. Will stay again, customer service is really good and friendly. 😀

Just out of the main town but makes up for it with lovely tree setting and excellent facilities. Pool, outdoor fire area, camp kitchen with TV, outdoor pizza oven. Busy place for a reason.
Only one night for us but would highly recommend

A bit out of town but nicely nestled into the gum trees. Great amenities nice clean easy access path. The only thing is if really high curbs to try and get vans into their parking spots….
Wi-Fi password doesn’t work

We found the park to be very noisy with passing traffic.... cars, trucks and motorbikes... as it is right on the major road to and from Clare. We also thought there could've been another toilet and shower block for the use of campers on the other side of the creek where we were camped.
It was great to be so close to the Riesling Trail for walking and cycling.

Booked a caravan site. Clean and well planned. Very friendly staff. Camp kitchens are also good. A tip though. When going to the toilet take a few gum leaves with you as they will be much better than the toilet paper supplied in the toilets. The cubical floor is littered with confetti paper evidence that you will even struggle get the .”glad wrap” toilet. thin paper out of the dispenser. Altogether a poor show for a quality park.
We stopped for a night on our way through, stayed in the grass area, looks a good location to do some exploring. Staff very friendly.
Lovey Family Caravan with fantastic customer service. Enjoyed our stay and we be back. Campsites and cabins where lovey (if you could put a bedside table in the 1 bedroom cabins it be outstanding)

We stayed in Clare 12th-14th July, the park was booked out and raining (which they can't control) but the ablutions were dirty from mud and ran out of soap, paper towels and toilet paper in one stall, roster on the back of the door had them cleaning it once a day but it really needed to be done twice a day.
A powered site . A really good park with excellent amenities, good ,helpful staff and competetive prices.
This a beautiful park with so much on offer families.  Reception and grounds staff super friendly.  Drive through sites gravel and very generous.  Definitely will return.
One of the nicest parks I’ve ever been to , beautiful soundings   Great facilities, clean and a amazing central cooking/entertaining are with huge open fire and pizza ovens to use
Top park, highly recommend 👌 👍
I didn't stay in a cabin but looked really good the caravan park in its self definitely  a place to go

Excellent park central to wineries in the Clare Valley... awesome facilities with x2 pools...bmx track... bouncy pillow.... fully stocked camp kitchen and bbq areas.... pizza ovens... we stayed June long weekend and they had live music with coffee and pizza vans... reception staff are super friendly set the tone for a great stay.... will definitely be back.

4th year now Discovery Parks in Clare for Clare Valley Gourmet Weekend and it’s been a great visit once again! Venue keeps getting better with heated pools and great facilities well looked after. Thank you to the team, see you next year!
Big camp site. Good service. Facilities ok. Only that the kitchen tables are in open room which is cold in cold days; however they must be perfect in warm days
Great spot
Great location with great activities for all ages.

Clean amenities with good kitchen/laundry.

Sites mostly gravel and reasonable large (10 x 7 mtr).
Good way out of town. Needs more amenities south of the creek

Great family park on huge grounds which have everything you can ask for….close to all the attractions.  Hire an electric bike and ride the Riesling trail….staff are delightful and very helpful….stunning part of the world…a visit to Martindale Hall is a must…
Great location and clean park.
Good location, just on the Highway heading into Clare (5mins away). Smaller cabin with amenities, nice and clean, a popular discovery park so it does get busy.

My wife and I, and 1 of our daughters, had a 3 night stay here in our caravan. The facilities are first rate. Camp kitchen has everything you could want. Lots of toilet and shower facilities throughout the area. These were clean every time we used them. Staff are very friendly. The powered sites are all a good size (Ours had enough room for the caravan, awning/annex of caravan and 2 cars). Perfectly located a short drive from Clare itself. Highly recommend this caravan park

Right on the main highway, trucks coming through all night, way too noisy to get any sleep, glad we were only there for the night. Couldn't sit outside the wasps were horrendous. I won't be going back in a hurry.

We had the most amazing time staying at Clare over the Easter period, the staff are amazing, kind, helpful, so many amazing planned activities and on site fun for the kiddies. One of the best family trips! So much to do in the Clare valley region. Thankyou to discovery parks Clare for your wonderful hospitality.
Great spot, spacious area for unpowered vehicles. Friendly team, clean facilities, we will be back!

Not sure on every day use but it's mainly the show grounds an used for the rodeos .
But there is a caravan park across the road......

Staff were unaccomodating for afternoon bike hire and after back and forth between the 3 staff for a simple enquiry then advised it was not possible to hire bikes without staying at the park. Referred another business which was closed.
Great spot close to brilliant wineries and just so convenient. Thanks to you all for a wonderful stay and good clean amenities too. Best wishes rich and lee
What a beautiful park, love the drive thru spots. Great pools (2)!!

It seems not all standard cabins are created equal. It would have been nice to have been given the option of a cabin with a balcony to sit on but apparently none were available for the four night we stayed. Minimal utensils for cooking meant we had to get creative with our menu planning. For a unit that could accommodate 5 people, the fridge was totally inadequate. No point in advertising no smoking rooms if other guests sit on their step and the smoke wafts across. I was prepared to give a higher rating until the Friday afternoon caravanners arrived in their droves and thought nothing of staying up and talking until 12:30am. Of course if you shut the office at 5pm, no one would know. It seems it’s my responsibility to phone the after hours number and complain! Not all guests are created equal either.  I can’t compare my spacious $200 a night house by the beach on the Fleurieu Peninsula, with its own private garden, to this overpriced dog box at $160 a night!
Amazing caravan park lots of stuff to do with the kids and the new waterpark is amazing highly recommend staying here very nice park
Beautiful park with good amenities and friendly staff. Great for families

First time stays at Discovery Parks-Clare.
We got number 1 room and with balcony.
Close to swimming pool and bike track.
We get to watch kids while they play on the jumping pillow.

When we arrived, the air con was on and makes the cabin feels welcome and comfortable. It was a warm afternoon that day.

Bathroom area looks like recent upgrade. Floor board looks new.
They have provided clean towels, soaps, hair dryers, extra toilet papers etc.
Kitchen area, lounge area looks presentable. Essential needs are all provided. Kettle, pots, electric cooker, toaster, knife, etc..
Extra blankets provided in the cupboard if needed.

Just the bedroom looks old with cupboard and shelves.
Another things is dirty as we don't normally wear shoes inside house. But that can't be avoided. Totally understand. They have provided the sponge mop but we didn't have floor liquid to wash the floor.

I have attached with photos.

The reason we came over was because of the new water park opened but kids did not go and play at the new park. Not sure why.
They are happy with swimming pool.

Overall happy with the stays.
Will explore other Discovery Park in the future.
Fantastic place, especially if you have kids. 2 pools and jumping pillow and new waterpark. We were upgraded to a larger cabin.

Was a wonderful place to stay for the few nights we had.  Mr 5 didn't want to leave! Beautifully maintained and serviced with all staff being friendly and  helpful, especially the people cleaning the pool, trying to get it up and running despite the wind and pollen for the eager kids first thing in the morning :)
Will def be back

Stayed here on a recent work trip.  Great cabin, clean and comfortable. What a great location.  We were able to walk to a couple of wineries.  And enjoyed our evenings drinking wine, and watching the kids staying at the park enjoying the pool, jumping pillow and bike track all from the comfort of our front porch.
Will definitely come back here on holidays and enjoy more of what Clare has to offer.
Awesome park, great facilities and family friendly. Really enjoyed the water park and the swimming heated pool x 2. They have a basketball court and pump track.
Nice spot.

Had a site other side of creek. Bbq area very rarely cleaned. Most times the cleaners come over and seem to just look at it but not clean it.Toilet block not well cleaned. Still dried toothpaste in sinks after cleaning done.

An uncramped park set in a beautiful location. The park has lots of grassed areas, a pool which is now open and large camp kitchen with wood fire in the colder months. Set on the edge of the clare township, its the perfect location to base yourself to explore numerous wineries, some within walking distance.

Discovery Parks - Clare in the stunning Clare Valley is an easy 1 1/2 hour drive from Adelaide. Set out on ambling bike rides or leisurely walks along the famous Riesling Trail, indulge in fresh produce and internationally renowned wines at the myriad of cellar doors, or set off on a day trip to enjoy all the attractions of the bustling capital.

The Discovery Parks – Clare is an ideal gateway to the Eyre Peninsula and the South Australian outback.

you can discover the 35 kilometre Riesling Trail, named after the region’s famed grape variety. Walk, ride or run past cellar doors, scenic landscapes, country vistas and rolling hills.

A fully equipped caravan park with plenty of powered and camping sites along with a range of deluxe cabin accommodation Discovery Parks - Clare has everyone covered!

The Clare caravan park was a great place to spend the night, all the amenities are great and cleaned regularly and the staff at the check in desk are friendly and very knowledgeable on the Local.

Very close to the wineries and can hire bikes there to get to them . Although there are a few within walking distance .
The toilets if you are camping are very small. Ideal for children but not for adults feels like you are going to sit on floor when using them

A good, yet pricey park in the Clare Valley. Renovations were well underway when we stayed on our powered site.  Hopefully finished before long. Amenities were clean but a little dated for the price.
Lawns were green on some sites, others were dry and brown. Many caravan sites were gravel only.
I seem to remember this park being better a decade ago.

The office girls have been extremely helpful. They accommodated us by creating a drive through and helping us extend our stay. Even though we were second row in from the road, the road noise is bearable and dies right off by the evening. Great park and is central to all the little historic towns. Thank you 🙂

Caravan park is in a nice location about 4klm out of town and is a nice park in general
However they do have a thing here called dynamic pricing (not peak and off peak) which means they can charge what they want for any given day based on supply and demand
When questioned they said this was a directive from head office yet this is the first discovery park where I have seen this applied so suggest you ask their prices as they are different for each day
Also a nice surprise for us was that the pool was closed but no mention on their website
Yes they are doing works around the area and there was no need to close the pool as it was a safer place than the jumping castle was and that was open
My recommendation is to shop around but if you must go here just be aware that they can charge what they like
For a normal three days (no public holidays or school holidays) we got slugged and extra 15+%
An awesome weekend! Service was top notch, the cabin was brilliant and clean, the pedal carts and facilities sensational. We will be back next year for sure!

Stayed in an economy cabin over 3 days. Comfortable bed, clean rooms and a great shower. Tucked away at the back away from road noise, passing internal traffic and table and chairs to sit outside under the trees and view the paddock next door with the build life well worth it.

Expensive for what you get and the price changes without notifying you.
Very difficult to get through on the phone and seems no point leaving a message
Been in much nicer Discovery Parks

Nice old park but needs a few improvements & road noise quite noisy, campsites a tad small.
Bathrooms were always kept clean & tidy. Music, Pizza & kombi keg were great for the school holidays

A lovely place to stay while exploring Clare Valley. We had two young kids with us 6,4 and they were sad to leave. A new pool is underway and a water play area. Hiring the go carts is fun and the bike pump track was a massive hit. Staff are so helpful and friendly and the tourism office next to the park has nice coffee and local craft.
Beautiful place to stay. Nice and clean accommodation,  friendly staff and well maintained lawns
Great caravan park close to all wineries and services in the town.
Seriously friendly and accommodating staff. Nice quiet spot.

Apart from no TV reception this was awesome. So quiet friendly staff (who lent us an aerial to try for tv but we couldn’t get it working) no drama though.
Clean amenities great staff only bummer was the big walk into town but was nice still
We love this place

Used a basic 2 bedroom cabin. Very clean, nice beds and clean bathroom. Stayed for 2 nights and walked my small dog around the park. Paid a visit to the wetlands in Clare near the golf course, good walk and nice views. Would recommend and will go again.
Very nice place will stay again

The park facilities were great. Kids loved the pump track and jumping pillow. Camp kitchen was great, the fire place and pizza ovens were a lovely touch.
However we stayed in a superior cabin and it was not good. It smelt awful, was dirty and beds were very uncomfortable.
Beautiful place to stay, clean and responsive staff, even after hours. Park was next to Clare Valley ℹ️ centre.

The people on the front desk were incredibly friendly. The place is very well located 3 minutes from the town.
Most of the cabins are around the playground so very good for families to let the kids go play. They have a path for scooters really cool and space for basketball and a playground near by. The open cooking area is very bright and nice to make your own dinner. They also have rental bikes in the front desk and the trail is very close by.
The cabins are basic as they all are but air conditioning works great and kitchen is just good for any need.
Beds are small and basic but that is for every park like this. The grounds are nice and have lots of green area around.

Basic amenities, nice location - you'll need transport in and out. It's a quiet spot. Rooms are cosy, bathroom can be a bit of a tight squeeze (if you're in the standard cabins). If you're only needing a place to rest whilst you're doing things during the day, then this is value for money.

Discussed a 1 night stay for 3 across an unpowered site while passing through on a motorbike tour this weekend, was a no exceptions taken 3 night minimum. The office confirmed that sites were available, when asked if we rolled up without calling ahead we'd have been refused also, when sites are available??
Seems Discovery Park are of the belief our money shouldn't be spent with a local restaurant for dinner, a taxi for a trip in and out for a beer, cafe for breakfast and a service station to fuel up.
We're now spending our money in the accommodating Auburn community.

Huge park that’s close to wineries! You can access the Riesling trail from here and they do bike hires. There are a couple wineries within walking distance. 5min walk is the lovely Shut the Gate winery and the information centre is basically touching camp. Great little centre for more wine, info and they had a food truck when we were there.

Great caravan park. 4th we have stayed in the unpowered sites.
Sites are nice and big.
Portable toilets and showers were new, not sure if they were for the Gourmet weekend or not.
Only down side is some clown was running his generator during the day.

If you camp @ the Clare discovery park MAKE SURE you get a gravelled site near the amenities as the ones over the creek are WET & with dripping trees & NO amenities except portable loos.. very unsuitable & expensive for bush camping type sites..

We started off camping which was fine until way too many huge 8-legged visitors decided to visit our camp (no fault of the park there had just been a lot of rain and it was an unlucky situation to find ourselves in). We decided the next day to just go home and upon telling the staff we'd be leaving early and why they were so kind of offer us a cabin instead so we could finish our stay for the weekend. It really made our day and was so very appreciated!!!! got to enjoy our saturday in the Clare Valley how it should be; lots of wine and good food!
Wonderful service!!! would definitely stay again!!
one of the best stay ever in regional places. Management and staff were very professional and friendly service. @Ricky(AM) 👍
Great spot with excellent amenities
